title: `oculus.test.ts` URL-parsing test uses retired writ-type literal `'brief'`

decisions.question: What placeholder replaces the literal `'brief'` at `packages/plugins/oculus/src/oculus.test.ts:316-317`?
decisions.rationale: No legacy in new stuff (#10) — opaque placeholder avoids reintroducing retired vocabulary, and aligning with the parent commission's selection keeps merge-order independence.

decisions.question: What does the implementer do if the parent commission (`w-mobzggpr-d37a4769915f`) has already applied the rename to `'bug'` before this commission runs?
decisions.rationale: Prefer delete to repair when the thing is derived/already-resolved (#38) — the target state is already achieved, so close cleanly rather than fabricate a redundant edit.

decisions.question: Does this commission extend beyond the single literal at lines 316-317 to audit other stale test-literal vocabulary in the Oculus package (or more broadly)?
decisions.rationale: MVP is the thinnest slice that answers the named question (#23) — the brief names one file and one literal; broader vocabulary audits are a separate concern and the parent already covers the repo-wide sweep.
